brief_justification: Correctly identifies C with valid supporting formula and reasoning; goes multiple levels deep — covers uniformity assumption, real-world birthday skew, lower-bound implication, and random-oracle model for hash functions; no explicit naming of own biases or mid-analysis course-correction, just linear exposition; verifies indirectly by testing all four answer choices against the chosen answer rather than applying a direct counterfactual or boundary check; acknowledges the uniformity assumption as an idealization and notes real-world deviation, but does not quantify confidence or state what would flip the conclusion; systematically addresses all three competing answers with specific reasons for rejection; formula is stated and assumptions are traced, but intermediate steps (e.g., why non-uniform distribution raises collision probability) are asserted rather than derived
